test_cases.description: Verifies that GoogleProvider is defined in the correct package and file, implements the Provider interface, and contains all required fields.
preconditions: The Provider interface is defined in internal/auth (spec 02)
test_cases.input: Compile and inspect the GoogleProvider type
test_cases.expected: GoogleProvider compiles successfully, satisfies the Provider interface (compile-time check), and has fields for client_id, client_secret, authorize_url, token_url, userinfo_url, scopes, and an injectable HTTP client
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode: var _ Provider = (*GoogleProvider)(nil) // compile-time interface check; assert no compile error

test_cases.description: Verifies that NewGoogleProvider sets all four defaults when the OAuthProvider config has empty URL fields.
preconditions: OAuthProvider config struct is available from internal/serverconfig
test_cases.input: cfg = OAuthProvider{Name: "google", ClientID: "id", ClientSecret: "secret", AuthorizeURL: "", TokenURL: "", UserInfoURL: ""}
test_cases.expected: Returned *GoogleProvider has AuthorizeURL == "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth", TokenURL == "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token", UserInfoURL == "https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo", and Scopes == "openid email profile"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
p = NewGoogleProvider(cfg)
assert p.AuthorizeURL == "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
assert p.TokenURL == "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
assert p.UserInfoURL == "https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo"
assert p.Scopes == "openid email profile" // or equivalent slice/string representation

test_cases.description: Verifies that NewGoogleProvider uses provided URL overrides instead of defaults when non-empty values are supplied in the config.
test_cases.input: cfg = OAuthProvider{Name: "google", ClientID: "id", ClientSecret: "secret", AuthorizeURL: "https://custom.example.com/auth", TokenURL: "https://custom.example.com/token", UserInfoURL: "https://custom.example.com/userinfo"}
test_cases.expected: Returned *GoogleProvider has AuthorizeURL == "https://custom.example.com/auth", TokenURL == "https://custom.example.com/token", UserInfoURL == "https://custom.example.com/userinfo"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
p = NewGoogleProvider(cfg)
assert p.AuthorizeURL == "https://custom.example.com/auth"
assert p.TokenURL == "https://custom.example.com/token"
assert p.UserInfoURL == "https://custom.example.com/userinfo"

test_cases.description: Verifies that SetHTTPClient replaces the internal HTTP client on GoogleProvider, enabling test injection.
test_cases.expected: After calling p.SetHTTPClient(customClient), the provider uses the injected client for subsequent HTTP requests (verified by observing requests hit the mock server)
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
p = NewGoogleProvider(defaultCfg)
mockServer = httptest.NewServer(mockHandler)
p.SetHTTPClient(mockServer.Client())
// make a call that triggers HTTP and confirm mockServer received the request
assert mockServer.receivedRequest == true

test_cases.description: Verifies that AuthorizeURL returns a correctly formed Google authorization URL with all required query parameters.
test_cases.expected: Returned URL starts with "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth" and contains query parameters: client_id=<id>, redirect_uri=http://localhost:8080/callback, response_type=code, scope=openid+email+profile (URL-encoded), state=random-state-string
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
url = p.AuthorizeURL("random-state-string", "http://localhost:8080/callback")
parsed = parseURL(url)
assert parsed.host+parsed.path == "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
assert parsed.queryParam("response_type") == "code"
assert parsed.queryParam("client_id") == p.ClientID
assert parsed.queryParam("redirect_uri") == "http://localhost:8080/callback"
assert parsed.queryParam("state") == "random-state-string"
assert parsed.queryParam("scope") contains "openid" AND "email" AND "profile"

test_cases.description: Verifies that ExchangeCode sends a POST with application/x-www-form-urlencoded body containing all required fields including grant_type=authorization_code and returns the access_token on success.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to return HTTP 200 with JSON body {"access_token": "tok123"}
p.SetHTTPClient configured to use the mock server's client
test_cases.expected: ExchangeCode returns ("tok123", nil); the mock server received a POST with Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded and body containing grant_type=authorization_code, client_id, client_secret, code=authcode, redirect_uri
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
token, err = p.ExchangeCode("authcode", "http://localhost:8080/callback")
assert err == nil
assert token == "tok123"
// inspect captured request at mock server:
assert capturedRequest.Method == "POST"
assert capturedRequest.Header["Content-Type"] == "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"
assert capturedRequest.FormValue("grant_type") == "authorization_code"
assert capturedRequest.FormValue("client_id") == p.ClientID
assert capturedRequest.FormValue("client_secret") == p.ClientSecret
assert capturedRequest.FormValue("code") == "authcode"
assert capturedRequest.FormValue("redirect_uri") == "http://localhost:8080/callback"

test_cases.description: Verifies that ExchangeCode returns an error containing the HTTP status code when the token endpoint returns a non-2xx response.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to return HTTP 401 with an error body
test_cases.expected: ExchangeCode returns ("", error) where error.Error() contains "401"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
token, err = p.ExchangeCode("bad-code", "http://localhost:8080/callback")
assert token == ""
assert err != nil
assert err.Error() contains "401"

test_cases.description: Verifies that ExchangeCode returns an error when the token endpoint response body is invalid JSON.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to return HTTP 200 with body "not-json"
test_cases.expected: ExchangeCode returns ("", error) with a non-nil error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
token, err = p.ExchangeCode("authcode", "http://localhost:8080/callback")
assert token == ""
assert err != nil

test_cases.description: Verifies that GetUserInfo sends a GET with Authorization: Bearer header to userinfo_url and returns a populated UserInfo on success.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to return HTTP 200 with valid Google userinfo JSON
p.SetHTTPClient configured to use the mock server
test_cases.expected: GetUserInfo returns (*UserInfo, nil) with non-empty ID, Login, Email, and Name fields; mock server received GET with Authorization: Bearer valid-token
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("valid-token")
assert err == nil
assert info != nil
assert info.ID != ""
assert info.Login != ""
assert info.Email != ""
// inspect captured request:
assert capturedRequest.Method == "GET"
assert capturedRequest.Header["Authorization"] == "Bearer valid-token"

test_cases.description: Verifies that GetUserInfo correctly maps Google userinfo response fields to the UserInfo struct fields.
preconditions: httptest.Server returns HTTP 200 with body: {"id":"117730543842840592312","email":"jane.doe+work@gmail.com","verified_email":true,"name":"Jane Doe"}
test_cases.expected: UserInfo.ID == "117730543842840592312", UserInfo.Login == "janedoework", UserInfo.Email == "jane.doe+work@gmail.com", UserInfo.Name == "Jane Doe"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("valid-token")
assert err == nil
assert info.ID == "117730543842840592312"
assert info.Login == "janedoework"
assert info.Email == "jane.doe+work@gmail.com"
assert info.Name == "Jane Doe"

test_cases.description: Verifies that GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) with the status code in the error message when userinfo endpoint returns non-2xx.
test_cases.expected: GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) where error.Error() contains "403"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("expired-token")
assert info == nil
assert err != nil
assert err.Error() contains "403"

test_cases.description: Verifies that GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) when the userinfo response body cannot be decoded as JSON.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to return HTTP 200 with body "<html>not json</html>"
test_cases.expected: GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) with a non-nil error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("valid-token")
assert info == nil
assert err != nil

test_cases.description: Verifies that the username derivation extracts the email local part, removes non-alphanumeric/non-hyphen characters, and truncates to 39 characters.
test_cases.expected: UserInfo.Login == "janedoework" which matches [0-9A-Za-z-]{1,39}
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("valid-token")
assert err == nil
assert info.Login == "janedoework"
assert len(info.Login) >= 1 AND len(info.Login) <= 39
assert info.Login matches regexp "^[0-9A-Za-z-]+$"

test_cases.description: Verifies that GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) when sanitization of the email local part yields an empty string.
preconditions: httptest.Server returns userinfo with email = "...@gmail.com" (local part contains only dots, which are stripped)
test_cases.expected: GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) with a descriptive non-nil error; no UserInfo is returned
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("valid-token")
assert info == nil
assert err != nil
assert err.Error() contains descriptive message about username derivation failure

test_cases.description: Verifies the canonical example: email jane.doe+work@gmail.com produces Login == janedoework.
preconditions: httptest.Server returns userinfo JSON with email = "jane.doe+work@gmail.com"
test_cases.expected: UserInfo.Login equals exactly "janedoework"
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("valid-token")
assert err == nil
assert info.Login == "janedoework"

test_cases.description: Verifies that cmd/af-hub/main.go contains a case "google" branch in the provider registration switch that calls auth.NewGoogleProvider.
preconditions: cmd/af-hub/main.go is updated with Google provider registration
test_cases.expected: The switch statement contains a `case "google":` branch that invokes `auth.NewGoogleProvider(cfg)` and registers the result with the Registry; the code compiles without errors under `go build ./cmd/af-hub/...`

test_cases.description: Verifies that when config.toml contains a google OAuth provider entry, the server registers it in the Registry under the name 'google' and exposes it via the providers API.
preconditions: config.toml contains [[oauth.providers]] entry with name="google", client_id="test-id", client_secret="test-secret"
Server is started with that config
test_cases.input: HTTP GET /api/providers (or equivalent providers API endpoint)
test_cases.expected: Response includes a provider entry with name == "google"; server starts without error
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
start server with googleConfig
response = httpGet("/api/providers")
assert response.statusCode == 200
assert response.body contains provider with name == "google"

test_cases.description: Verifies that docs/configuration.md contains a Google OAuth section with required subsections and a config.toml example.
test_cases.expected: File contains a section titled "Google OAuth" with content covering: Google Cloud Console OAuth 2.0 Client ID creation, OAuth consent screen configuration, authorized redirect URIs, and a config.toml example with name="google", client_id, and client_secret
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
content = readFile("docs/configuration.md")
assert content contains "Google OAuth"
assert content contains "Google Cloud Console"
assert content contains "OAuth consent screen"
assert content contains "redirect"
assert content contains 'name = "google"'
assert content contains "client_id"
assert content contains "client_secret"

test_cases.description: Verifies that the test file internal/auth/google_provider_test.go exists and all tests pass under go test.
test_cases.expected: All tests pass with exit code 0; test file covers: successful token exchange, token endpoint non-2xx, successful userinfo retrieval, userinfo endpoint non-2xx, username derivation (including truncation and empty-result error), and URL override via config
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = runCommand("go test ./internal/auth/...")
assert result.exitCode == 0
assert result.output contains "PASS"
assert result.output does not contain "FAIL"

test_cases.description: Verifies that the test suite asserts grant_type=authorization_code is present in the token exchange POST body sent to the mock server.
preconditions: internal/auth/google_provider_test.go is implemented with a mock httptest.Server for the token endpoint
The mock server captures the incoming request body
test_cases.input: Call p.ExchangeCode("code", "http://localhost/callback") with mock server active
test_cases.expected: The mock server's captured request body contains grant_type=authorization_code; the test assertion for this field passes

property_tests.description: For any Google userinfo response where GetUserInfo returns a non-nil *UserInfo, the Login field must be non-empty and match usernameRegexp [0-9A-Za-z-]{1,39}.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary email addresses whose local parts (before @) contain at least one alphanumeric or hyphen character; for each, construct a mock userinfo JSON response with id and name fields, call GetUserInfo, and check the Login field
property_tests.invariant_check: if info, err = GetUserInfo(token); err == nil AND info != nil { assert len(info.Login) >= 1 AND len(info.Login) <= 39 AND info.Login matches regexp '^[0-9A-Za-z-]+$' }

property_tests.description: For any GoogleProvider constructed via NewGoogleProvider with empty URL fields, the three endpoint URLs always equal the documented Google defaults.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary OAuthProvider structs with empty AuthorizeURL, TokenURL, and UserInfoURL fields (but varying ClientID and ClientSecret values), call NewGoogleProvider, and inspect the resulting URLs
property_tests.invariant_check:
p = NewGoogleProvider(cfg where AuthorizeURL=="", TokenURL=="", UserInfoURL=="")
assert p.AuthorizeURL == "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"
assert p.TokenURL == "https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token"
assert p.UserInfoURL == "https://www.googleapis.com/oauth2/v2/userinfo"

property_tests.description: For any call to ExchangeCode, the HTTP POST body sent to the token endpoint always contains grant_type=authorization_code, regardless of the code or redirectURI values.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary (code, redirectURI) pairs as strings; for each, call ExchangeCode against a mock server that captures the request body and records whether grant_type=authorization_code is present

property_tests.description: For any email address whose local part contains at least one alphanumeric or hyphen character, the username derivation terminates with a result of length 1-39 containing only [0-9A-Za-z-], and the derivation process is bounded (no unbounded loop or allocation).
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary email strings with at least one alphanumeric or hyphen character in the local part (before @). Apply the sanitization and truncation logic directly (via a white-box helper or by calling GetUserInfo with a mock server). Measure number of iterations if looping is used.
property_tests.invariant_check:
derivedLogin = deriveUsername(email)
assert len(derivedLogin) >= 1
assert len(derivedLogin) <= 39
assert derivedLogin matches regexp '^[0-9A-Za-z-]+$'
// Termination: if sanitization uses a loop, assert iterations <= len(localPart) (bounded by input length)

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that NewGoogleProvider constructs and returns a *GoogleProvider without panicking or returning an error when client_id or client_secret is empty.
edge_case_tests.expected: NewGoogleProvider returns a non-nil *GoogleProvider with default URLs applied; no panic or compile error occurs
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
p = NewGoogleProvider(OAuthProvider{Name: "google", ClientID: "", ClientSecret: ""})
assert p != nil
assert p.AuthorizeURL == "https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth"

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that ExchangeCode returns a timeout-related error and does not leak goroutines when the token endpoint hangs beyond the HTTP client timeout.
preconditions: httptest.Server configured to never respond (hangs indefinitely)
Injected HTTP client has a very short timeout (e.g., 10ms)
edge_case_tests.expected: ExchangeCode returns ("", error) where error is a context deadline or timeout error; call completes within ~2x the client timeout; no goroutine leak detectable via goleak or runtime.NumGoroutine comparison
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
client = &http.Client{Timeout: 10ms}
p.SetHTTPClient(client)
start = now()
token, err = p.ExchangeCode("authcode", "http://localhost/callback")
elapsed = now() - start
assert token == ""
assert err != nil
assert err contains "timeout" OR "deadline"
assert elapsed < 500ms // bounded termination

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that ExchangeCode returns the underlying net/http error and does not leave partial state when the connection to the token endpoint is refused.
preconditions: p configured with token_url pointing to a port where nothing is listening (connection refused)
edge_case_tests.expected: ExchangeCode returns ("", error) with a non-nil network-level error; no partial state is retained
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
p = NewGoogleProvider(OAuthProvider{TokenURL: "http://127.0.0.1:1/token", ...})
token, err = p.ExchangeCode("authcode", "http://localhost/callback")
assert token == ""
assert err != nil
assert err contains "connection refused" OR "dial"

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that GetUserInfo returns a timeout-related error and does not leak goroutines when the userinfo endpoint hangs beyond the HTTP client timeout.
preconditions: Injected HTTP client has a very short timeout (e.g., 10ms)
edge_case_tests.expected: GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) where error contains timeout/deadline information; call completes within bounded time
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
client = &http.Client{Timeout: 10ms}
p.SetHTTPClient(client)
start = now()
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("valid-token")
elapsed = now() - start
assert info == nil
assert err != nil
assert err contains "timeout" OR "deadline"
assert elapsed < 500ms // bounded termination

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that GetUserInfo returns the underlying net/http error when the connection to userinfo_url is refused at the network layer.
preconditions: p configured with userinfo_url pointing to a port with nothing listening
edge_case_tests.expected: GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) with a non-nil network-level error; response body is closed if partially received
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
p = NewGoogleProvider(OAuthProvider{UserInfoURL: "http://127.0.0.1:1/userinfo", ...})
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("valid-token")
assert info == nil
assert err != nil
assert err contains "connection refused" OR "dial"

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that the derived username is truncated to exactly 39 characters when the sanitized email local part exceeds 39 characters.
preconditions: httptest.Server returns userinfo with email = "aaaaabbbbbcccccdddddeeeeefffff12345678901@example.com" (local part sanitizes to 41+ alphanumeric chars)
edge_case_tests.expected: UserInfo.Login is exactly 39 characters long and contains only characters matching [0-9A-Za-z-]
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
// email local part: "aaaaabbbbbcccccdddddeeeeefffff12345678901" -> 41 chars after sanitization
info, err = p.GetUserInfo("valid-token")
assert err == nil
assert len(info.Login) == 39
assert info.Login matches regexp "^[0-9A-Za-z-]{39}$"
assert info.Login == "aaaaabbbbbcccccdddddeeeeefffff123456789" // first 39 chars

edge_case_tests.description: Verifies that GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) when the email has no @ character or the local part before @ is empty.
preconditions: httptest.Server returns userinfo with email = "invalidemail" (no @ sign) in one test variant, and email = "@gmail.com" (empty local part) in another
edge_case_tests.expected: GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) for both email variants with a non-nil descriptive error

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end smoke test of a successful Google OAuth login flow using mock HTTP servers for the Google token and userinfo endpoints.
smoke_tests.trigger: Simulate full OAuth callback: callback handler receives code and state, calls GoogleProvider.ExchangeCode then GetUserInfo, creates or updates user record, and issues a session token
mockable: Google token endpoint (replaced by httptest.Server returning {"access_token": "test-tok"})
Google userinfo endpoint (replaced by httptest.Server returning valid userinfo JSON with email, id, name)
expected_effects: ExchangeCode returns a non-empty access token
GetUserInfo returns *UserInfo with ID=="117730543842840592312", Login=="janedoework", Email=="jane.doe+work@gmail.com", Name=="Jane Doe"
User record is created or updated in the database with provider_id, username, email, full_name
Session token is issued and HTTP redirect to dashboard is sent
No errors are returned from any step

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test of the error path where the Google user's email local part sanitizes to an empty string, resulting in GetUserInfo returning an error and the callback handler returning HTTP 502.
smoke_tests.trigger: Simulate OAuth callback where userinfo endpoint returns an email whose local part contains only disallowed characters (e.g. "...@gmail.com"); callback handler calls GetUserInfo and handles the error
mockable: Google token endpoint (returns valid access token)
expected_effects: ExchangeCode returns a non-empty access token without error
GetUserInfo returns (nil, error) with a descriptive message about username derivation failure
Callback handler responds with HTTP 502 to the client
No user record is created in the database

smoke_tests.description: Smoke test of server startup with a Google provider entry in config.toml, verifying it is registered in the Registry and available via the providers API.
smoke_tests.trigger: Start the af-hub server with a config.toml containing [[oauth.providers]] name="google", client_id="test-client-id", client_secret="test-client-secret"
mockable: Actual Google OAuth endpoints (not contacted during startup)
Database (can use in-memory SQLite or test fixture)
expected_effects: Server starts without any error or panic
Registry contains a GoogleProvider registered under the name "google"
GET /api/providers (or equivalent) returns a response that includes a provider entry with name=="google"
No connection to actual Google servers is made during startup
